summary: spec gate round 1 BLOCKED — oracle PASS, builder FAIL, architect FAIL; two coupled defects on the drain's class-scoping and its dedupe horizon

detail: the classify routing table read as an either/or (blocking to the graph, backlog to a forge issue) while every drain scenario was class-agnostic — an implementation exempting blocking follow-ups from the drain would have passed the whole suite; corrected to state the class decides graph admission, not filing, plus a covering scenario

dedupe was specified against open issues alone, so a follow-up whose filed issue had since closed would be re-filed on the retry path — the append-only record can carry no filed-state, so the dedupe horizon must span closed issues too; corrected with a covering scenario

summary: spec gate round 2 BLOCKED — oracle PASS, builder PASS (both round-1 defects closed), architect FAIL on a pre-existing passive scenario left unreconciled beside the new propose/admit split

detail: the pre-existing follow-up scenario names no actor for re-entry, so the boundary between filing and opening a change request was never asserted; closed additively with a covering scenario rather than by rewording the frozen scenario — its passive guarantee stays true, so no narrowing and no re-open

summary: spec gate round 3 BLOCKED — oracle PASS, builder PASS, architect FAIL on the dedupe horizon asserted inconsistently across the diff; round-2 additive closure confirmed to hold

detail: the round-1 dedupe-horizon fix was propagated to the detailed prose only, leaving the use-cases row and the fixed-universal governance paragraph still asserting open-only dedupe — the governance a producer loads first contradicted the frozen scenario it governs; all four mentions reconciled in one pass, including one the judge missed

summary: impl gate round 1 — all 21 frozen scenarios PASS individually, but IMPLEMENTATION_PASS false on an orthogonal structural read: the new write act was never admitted by the consumers that enumerate the kind list

detail: the ledger gained a 7th kind but the canonical write-ownership matrix still enumerated six, so the governance every actor loads to answer whether the conductor may write it silently said no — the doctrine's unconditional act blocked by the contract that grants it; the headless agent's write allowlist omitted it too, in exactly the unattended case the design exists for; synced ownership-governance, sdd-automaton, provenance-model and the conductor README

summary: impl gate round 2 IMPLEMENTATION_PASS true — 21/21 frozen scenarios independently re-derived, round-1 structural blockers confirmed closed; flagged one non-blocking enumeration straggler plus a pre-existing five-kinds staleness

detail: the consumer sweep claimed every enumeration was reconciled while three remained — the Scanner's account of the conductor's shard writes, the conductor's own autonomy-bar bullet, and the governance README still at five kinds (stale since the leash CR); a sweep that reports done while incomplete is the exact defect this CR specifies against, so the claim was made true rather than narrowed
